{{Quote|No, I'm not the Wookiee you're looking for.|translated=1|Rorworr attempts to fool a bounty hunter|<ref name="RPGCRB">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]''</ref>}}
'''Rorworr''', born in [[49 BBY/Legends|49 BBY]], was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] who lived on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] with [[Rorworr's father|his father]], an [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]]. With help from his [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]] [[TD0-2]], Rorworr studied at the [[Royal House of Learning/Legends|Royal House of Learning]] in Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], and often explored the wilderness areas surrounding the city. The Wookiee had a penchant for adventure, and assisted the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Rann I-Kanu]] with the investigation and defeat of a group of [[Veermok/Legends|veermok]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]].

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Rorworr helped defend Naboo during the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] by [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] forces. He served as a member of [[Naboo Resistance]] and the [[Naboo Underground]], freed several military and civilian [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], and helped [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala's]] forces infiltrate Theed undetected. During the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|battle]] itself, the Wookiee assisted in diverting enemy [[Troop/Legends|troops]] from Amidala's forces, defeated a [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droid]] in Theed's [[Palace Plaza/Legends|Palace Plaza]], and intercepted a [[Counterfeit Treaty of Naboo|counterfeit treaty]] forged by the Trade Federation.

When Naboo had been liberated from the Trade Federation, Rorworr continued to serve the people of Naboo. He helped defeat [[Savor Kibbs]] and aided in establishing a colony on Naboo's largest [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]]. Rorworr later became an agent of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and served [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] as a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] in a variety of missions until the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

After the formation of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Wookiee served as an agent and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], gunrunning and gathering military intelligence. After intercepting Imperial [[High Inquisitor]] [[Volytar]] at the [[Battle of Skorrupon]], Rorworr became known among the Rebellion as one of the heroes of Skorrupon.

Once the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was founded, however, Rorworr left military service to become an independent explorer and an adventurer. He made a name for himself as he contested the exploitation of wilderness planets and their native [[Species/Legends|species]].

==Biography==
===Youth on Naboo===
{{Quote|I have no idea what their names are, but I overheard their leader say they were planning to capture veermoks and sell them offworld. I'm quite sure that's illegal!|Tasrah Boh|<ref name="TP&SON">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=rpg_fastPlay_intro|text=The Smugglers of Naboo|int=The Smugglers of Naboo}} and ''[[The Predators]]''</ref>}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]]<ref name="IoTAG" /> born in [[49 BBY/Legends|49 BBY]]<ref name="49BBY" /> on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], Rorworr was brought to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] as a [[Youngling/Legends|child]] when [[Rorworr's father|his father]] was assigned to act as an [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] between the two [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. His father's station allowed Rorworr to attend the [[Royal House of Learning/Legends|Royal House of Learning]] in Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Theed/Legends|Theed]],<ref name="RIS" /> assisted by his [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]], [[TD0-2]].<ref name="AGRB">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]] Rulebook</ref> It was here that the Wookiee began learning to [[Pilot/Legends|fly]] [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]], practicing with local [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighters]].<ref name="RIS" /> He also befriended several other students while attending classes, including [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Sia-Lan Wezz]]; [[Dané]], a young girl who was secretly training to become a [[Handmaiden/Legends|handmaiden]]; [[Galak]], a volunteer in the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]; [[Arani Korden]],<ref name="IoTAG" /> Theed-born daughter of a [[House Korden]] [[Noble/Legends|noble]];<ref name="GOI">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref> and [[Deel Surool]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] whose wealthy parents set up a trading emporium on Naboo.<ref name="IoTAG" />

Young Rorworr had a love for exploring the wilderness beyond Theed, and often slipped away from school for several days at a time. The Wookiee's curiosity led him to explore many places hidden away from the city.<ref name="RIS" /> Occasionally, he came across equally curious [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Scout/Legends|explorers]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of amphibious natives that he found to be interesting.<ref name="SPIOT">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sketchGallery_1|text=Sneak Preview: ''Star Wars Invasion of Theed Adventure Game''}}</ref> While on a field trip,<ref name="IoTAG" /> he found a friend in the young [[Toba]],<ref name="SPIOT" /> a daring Gungan who was the eldest son of a wealthy [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] family.<ref name="Toba" /> Rorworr also had a fondness for [[Star/Legends|stargazing]] on his excursions.<ref name="RIS" />

{{RPG|"[[The Smugglers of Naboo|Smugglers of Naboo Fast-Play Game]]"}}
At some point, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ali-Vor]] brought his Padawan, [[Rann I-Kanu]], to Naboo in order to further his Padawan's training. Ali-Vor enrolled I-Kanu in the Royal House of Learning and regularly assigned tasks to his apprentice. While Ali-Vor was investigating poachers who were [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[Veermok/Legends|veermok]] off-planet, he asked I-Kanu to investigate rumors that some of the smugglers were operating out of a [[Saidle Frex's base|secret base]] near Theed, although Ali-Vor had not considered the rumors to be true. I-Kanu enlisted the aid of Rorworr, Galak, and Korden, friends he had made while at the Royal House of Learning, to help with the investigation. Using a map acquired by one of Korden's contacts, Rorworr guided the team to a ruined jungle shrine. The group infiltrated the hidden base found near the shrine, defeated the smugglers and their leader [[Saidle Frex]], and rescued [[Tasrah Boh]], a captured [[Professor/Legends|professor]] of [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeology]] from the House of Learning.<ref name="TP&SON" />

===Invasion of Naboo===
{{Quote|Less tax equals more profit.|Raymas Daal|<ref name="SI">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20010809a|text=Signal Interruption|int=Signal Interruption}}</ref>}}
{{RPG|"[[Signal Interruption]]"}}
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], the governing body of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], passed [[Senate Resolution BR-0371|legislation]] calling for the taxation of [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]] to outlying [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. A trade dispute soon developed between the Republic and a megacorporation known as the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], resulting in the [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of Naboo.<ref name="E1TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> At an early point in the blockade, Rorworr and a few other students met with [[Raymas Daal]], a small-time criminal, in the Naboo city of [[Kwilaan (city)|Kwilaan]]. Daal had a new backer, and offered the team 10,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to temporarily deactivate a Naboo [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]] located just outside of the city. Daal told the students he simply wanted to evade taxes on a rare [[Spice/Legends|spice]] shipment in order to increase profits, and intended to land while the [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] was deactivated. In reality, Daal's unnamed backer was the Trade Federation, which wanted to move [[Jammer/Legends|jamming]] [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] into Naboo [[Realspace/Legends|space]] undetected in preparation for an [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion of the planet]]. The meeting was interrupted by plain-clothes members of the Royal Naboo Security Forces, who intended to apprehend Daal for questioning; Daal, however, escaped, as did Rorworr and his companions. Fulfilling Daal's task, the students located the target array using information provided by Daal, and, bypassing four [[Security Guard/Legends|Security Guards]] and a pair of [[Technician/Legends|technicians]], disabled it. They returned to Kwilaan to locate Daal and collect their fee.<ref name="SI" />

{{RPG|"[[Peril on Naboo]]"}}
A short time later, Rorworr and a group consisting of Sia-Lan Wezz, Deel Surool, Dané, Galak, and Arani Korden<ref name="FF91">{{FactFile|91|TRA 17-18|The Trade Federation Attack}}</ref> booked passage off-world on the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Crescent (freighter)|''Crescent'']], [[Captain/Legends|captained]] by [[Nuun Pargen]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Immediately after taking off from Theed, Trade Federation [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] fired upon the freighter, wounding Nuun's daughter, [[Inea Pargen]]. Nuun was forced to perform an emergency landing on the [[Great Grass Plains/Legends|Great Grass Plains]]. Captain Pargen tried to repair the freighter, and sent Rorworr and his friends back into Theed to procure medical supplies for Inea's recovery.<ref name="PoNA1">{{WizCite|book=Secrets of Naboo|story=Peril on Naboo}} Act 1: The Invasion</ref>

[[File:Wookiee_a.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A young Rorworr and his translator droid, TD0-2]]
However, the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] began to invade the palatial city just as the crew acquired their supplies. During the course of their escape from Theed, the team encountered resistance from [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] and [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]]. They helped a family of Naboo nobles balance luggage on an overloaded [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] to escape the city safely, freed a collection of civilians and Security Guards from a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] escort, and resolved a firefight involving outnumbered Security Forces. They avoided the [[Main Boulevard]] Bridge, where the bulk of the invading forces filtered into the city, as well as the [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]]. Eventually, they encountered one [[Chee Mobok]], a frightened [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] textiles [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] who spoke very little [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. After calming Mobok down, the crew learned that he had crashed an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] into a nearby pond. Together, they fished the speeder out of the pond and used it to effect the rest of their escape.<ref name="PoNA1" />

Rorworr piloted the speeder along a tributary of the [[Solleu River/Legends|Solleu River]], over the occupied Main Boulevard Bridge, and out onto the river proper. The Federation forces on the bridge fired at the speeder, and three [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|Single Trooper Aerial Platforms]] broke from the battle to pursue the group. During the chase, Rorworr's maneuvers caused one STAP to crash into a [[Multi-Troop Transport/Legends|Multi-Troop Transport]], disabling the transport's cooling fins and sinking it in the river. After shaking the remaining STAPs, the group abandoned their battered speeder in [[Lianorm Swamp/Legends|Lianorm Swamp]].<ref name="PoNA1" />

Mobok was soon mauled by a veermok. When the group defeated the creature, they found themselves surrounded by a Gungan patrol. Led by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Mokem (Gungan)|Mokem]], the patrol escorted Rorworr's group to the borders of Lianorm. They followed the edge of the swamp, intending to rendezvous with Captain Pargen; however, when the group arrived, they found that the ''Crescent'' had already departed. In its place, they found a group of [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] fighting a small force of Naboo Security. Rorworr's group intervened, defeating the pirates, but only a lone Security Guard, [[Mett Habble/Legends|Mett Habble]], survived the firefight.<ref name="PoNA1" />

====Resistance and Underground====
{{Dialogue|Sia-Lan|There's so much going on in the Force. Neimoidian troops are ''everywhere!''<nowiki />|Rorworr|line2=no|"ARROOWAKH-WAA!"|TD0-2| 'Except in our wake,' the young master says.|attr=Sia-Lan Wezz and Rorworr discuss logistics, as TD0-2 translates|src=<ref name="BfT" />}}
Habble was one of [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Sio Bibble/Legends|Sio Bibble's]] disguised agents, and also the organizer of the recently formed [[Naboo Resistance]]. Habble guided the group to the Resistance camp, encouraging Rorworr to fly the speeder while he questioned the group about their escape from Theed. Rorworr's team parted ways with Chee Mobok when they arrived at the camp. Habble informed the passengers from the ''Crescent'' that although Theed and the Royal Palace had been captured by the forces of Trade Federation [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], Naboo's [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]] had escaped the planet in the company of a pair of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. After a brief respite, Habble invited the crew to a meeting with two other Resistance leaders, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Nathaniel Camaran]] and [[Oti Trinta]]. During the meeting, the crew joined the Resistance.<ref name="PoNA2">{{WizCite|book=Secrets of Naboo|story=Peril on Naboo}} Act 2: The Naboo Underground</ref>
{{Endgame}}
Rorworr and several fellow students from the Royal House of Learning were placed in the charge of [[Lucos Dannt]]; the former headmaster of the school was a leader of the [[Naboo Underground]], a cell of the Resistance that operated in and around Theed.<ref name="Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]], Adventure 1: Battle Droid Invasion</ref> Rorworr's first-hand knowledge of the Naboo countryside proved invaluable to the Underground as the group attempted to undermine the Trade Federation's occupation.<ref name="RIS" />

While scouting Theed, Rorworr, Galak, Surool, and Wezz encountered a patrol of four [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. The group attempted to flee, but became cornered in an alley containing STAP wreckage and containers of speeder parts. The group fought and defeated the droids, after which Rorworr discovered a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] in the STAP wreckage that contained the location of a Trade federation internment camp and a list of the Naboo leaders imprisoned there.<ref name="AGRB" />

{{RPG|''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]''}}
Rorworr participated in several prison breaks over the course of the next [[Standard week/Legends|week]]. The Underground had learned that a group of recently captured RSF pilots and guards was being held in a lightly guarded building near Theed's central [[Palace Plaza/Legends|Palace Plaza]] and that the prisoners' confiscated weapons were being stored near the facility. The prisoners were scheduled to soon be relocated to a more heavily guarded facility. Dannt determined that the opportunity to free the high-value prisoners and bolster the ranks of the resistance could not be missed. Since all the other, more experienced Underground teams were already in the field, Dannt assigned the [[Theed Palace Plaza Prison Break|task of jailbreaking the facility]] to his former disciples. Rorworr and a posse of other students infiltrated Theed and reached the target building, where they quickly dispatched a pair of [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] guards. The group broke into both the holding cells and the neighboring armory, issuing confiscated weaponry to the seven prisoners. As the students attempted to escort the prisoners to safety, the group was ambushed by a small force of Trade Federation [[OOM-series security droid/Legends|OOM security battle droids]]. The Underground team routed the reinforcements and led the prisoners to the Underground's headquarters. Many of the prisoners joined the Underground, strengthening the resistance movement.<ref name="IoTA2" />

While Rorworr's team was recuperating from their previous mission, Dannt introduced the students to [[Lialla Tane]], a resistance operative. Tane explained that she had heard of the students' exploits and requested their help. She had learned that her parents, [[Minister of Culture/Legends|Minister of Culture]] [[Kyu Tane]] and his wife, were being held by the Trade Federation in a prison camp on the [[Street of Glory]] very near the Theed Royal Palace. Tane was concerned for the health of her parents, since the Trade Federation wasn't feeding any of their prisoners. Tane, who was to participate in another mission, asked the students to [[Street of Glory Prison Break|free her parents]]. She provided information about the opposition the team would face, including the presence of a battle droid patrol in the area. Lialla advised the team that the prison consisted of unbreakable portable energy walls, and that a pair of Neimoidians overseeing the facility were able to deactivate an energy gate with a keypad.<ref name="IoTA3">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]], Adventure 3: Rescue</ref>

When the students arrived on the Street of Glory outside of the prison, they approached and negotiated with the Neimoidian overseers, who easily parted with the gate code, before turning their attention to the pair of Security Droids guarding the gate. After dispatching the droid guards, the students opened the prison. The cell itself housed the Tane family, four Naboo citizens, and one wounded RSF pilot. All of the prisoners were skeptical of their rescuers, and only the Tane family initially agreed to escape. A patrol of four battle droids arrived at the scene, alerted by the Neimoidians, and the students were forced to defeat the patrol before the remaining prisoners were convinced that they could be kept safe during the escape. The students escorted the prisoners back to the Naboo Underground, reuniting Lialla and a number of other Underground operatives with their families.<ref name="IoTA3" />

However, Rorworr and his classmates were eventually captured. They were held in [[Prison Compound 32]], a Trade Federation prison camp that was built from portable energy walls within the city of Theed. The group was imprisoned with [[Ruto Graven]], who was [[Assistant Minister of Internal Affairs]] for Queen Amidala; [[Private/Legends|Private]] [[Boraso]], an RSF Security Guard; and the [[Artist/Legends|artist]] [[Ela Sivel]]. Boraso, who had been wounded in battle, had hidden a set of security tools in his belt when he was captured, but did not have the expertise to use the tools.<ref name="IoTA3a">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]], Adventure 3A: Escape</ref>

While talking with the other prisoners, the team came under very brief scrutiny by a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord]], [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]. Soon after, the students [[Escape from Prison Compound 32|attempted to escape]] by distracting the pair of OOM security battle droids guarding the energy gate while they covertly disabled the gate's control console. The students disabled the gate and grabbed their weapons and gear that was stored in a crate just outside the prison entrance. Rorworr's group defeated the immediate guards and held off a squad of four B1 battle droids while their cellmates escaped.<ref name="IoTA3a" />
{{Endgame}}

At another point during the occupation, Rorworr, Surool, Wezz, and Korden reentered Theed, sprung a Trade Federation trap, and took cover in an alley. Planning to escape through a door at the end of the alley, Rorworr, Korden, and Wezz provided a diversion while Surool attempted to [[Slicer/Legends|slice]] the door's electronic lock. The fighters surprised a unit of B1 battle droids and held off their reinforcements. Rorworr's team began to retreat down the alley when a pair of droidekas rolled up. Surool opened the door, and all four students were surprised by what they saw on the other side.<ref name="BfT">"[[Battle for Theed]]". The three-page teaser comic ends without revealing what is on the other side of the door.</ref>
[[File:WookieeStereotype.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rorworr holds the line during a mission on Naboo.]]

====Reclaiming Naboo====
{{Quote|Glad you're here. We got some good news: Queen Amidala has returned. Not only does she have a couple of Jedi Knights with her, but she's somehow convinced the Gungans to fight along side us—''and'' she's got a plan.|Mett Habble|<ref name="PoNE" />}}
{{RPG|"[[Peril on Naboo]]"}}
Queen Amidala, after failing to persuade the Galactic Senate to intervene and end the Trade Federation's invasion, returned to Naboo. When Amidala requested that Mett Habble bring his best people to a planning session, Habble summoned Rorworr and his team to participate. The group followed Habble through Lianorm Swamp to the landing site of the Queen's [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|J-type 327 Nubian royal starship]]. They were greeted by the Queen and her handmaidens; Captain [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]], head of the Royal Naboo Security Forces; [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Nass]], leader of the Gungans; [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], a Gungan; two Jedi, [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]; their [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] ward from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]; and various Gungan and Underground [[General/Legends|generals]].<ref name="PoNA2" />

During the planning session, Amidala announced her plan to enter Theed via a secret passage. The leaders decided that using a Trade Federation Troop Transport would be the ideal way to approach the passage undetected, but realized that acquiring one would be difficult. At this point, Rorworr's team spoke up, volunteering information on the MTT that sank into the Solleu during their initial escape from Theed. With this information, the leaders formalized the main attack plan.<ref name="PoNA2" />

Boss Nass volunteered his Gungan [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]], the [[Gungengineeren]], to repair and salvage the submerged transport; however, his Gungan scouts were unable to locate the MTT. Queen Amidala tasked Rorworr's team with escorting the Gungengineeren to the site because the crew from the ''Crescent'' knew the exact location of the transport. The unit was also assigned to guard the Gungengineeren during the operation, since the Queen wanted to reassure the Gungans that the Naboo were committed to mutual cooperation. Rorworr and two others piloted three unfamiliar [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|tribubble bongos]], docking with a [[Hydrostatic bubble/Legends|hydrostatic bubble]] to rendezvous with [[Koh Pa Wupps]] and his team of Gungengineers. Loading the engineers and their tools into the [[Submarine/Legends|submersibles]], Wupps directed the group around [[Lake Paonga/Legends|Lake Paonga]], up the lower Solleu River, and through an underwater passage at the base of the [[Virdugo Plunge/Legends|Virdugo Plunge]] that led to the upper Solleu, dodging a group of [[Opee sea killer/Legends|opee sea killers]] along the way.<ref name="PoNA2" />

When the group located the downed MTT, the Gungengineeren began work on transporting it upriver for repair while Rorworr's team patrolled the area in the bongos. The site was attacked by a young, orphaned [[Colo claw fish/Legends|colo claw fish]], which Rorworr's team defeated through piloting skill with the unarmed submersibles.<ref name="PoNA2" />

====Battle for Theed====
{{Main|Battle of Naboo/Legends}}
{{Quote|Transport seven-one-four: We have you listed as sinking into the river during the invasion. How did you get to the lower plateau?|PDA-2, puzzled and slightly annoyed|<ref name="PoNE" />}}
Rorworr piloted the retrofitted MTT, [[Transport 714]], to the secret passage at the base of Theed's cliff wall, where the team unloaded Amidala, who was disguised as a handmaiden; the handmaiden [[Sabé/Legends|Sabé]], who was disguised as the Queen; Captain Panaka; the Jedi Jinn and Kenobi; Skywalker; the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]; and three dozen Naboo [[Troop/Legends|troops]]. Rorworr and his team were assigned to guard the [[Transport/Legends|transport]] and to monitor Federation radio traffic using its transponder.<ref name="PoNA3">{{WizCite|book=Secrets of Naboo|story=Peril on Naboo}} Act 3: Assault on Theed</ref>

When the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]] drew the Trade Federation forces away from Theed, Transport 714 was contacted by [[PDA-2]], the [[OOM command battle droid/Legends|OOM command battle droid]] assigned to remain behind and guard the Palace. Using a jury-rigged [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]], Rorworr's team tricked PDA-2 into believing that the transport was too badly damaged to move under its own power. PDA-2 overrode the transport's manual controls and remotely piloted it to [[Theed Hangar/Legends|Theed Hangar's]] repair bay, within the city. The unit ambushed the forces of [[ADO-8]], a command battle droid guarding the repair bay. Rorworr's team once again briefly encountered Darth Maul, who quickly left the repair bay in search of Jinn and Kenobi.<ref name="PoNA3" />

{{RPG|''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]''}}
The party found their way outside, in front of Theed's Royal Palace, where they encountered Sabé—still disguised as the Queen—and a small escort of Security Guards and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. Sabé requested the crew's assistance in creating a diversion that would allow them to enter the Palace. The team engaged an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]], a single STAP patrol, and a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of eight battle droids, distracting them long enough to allow Sabé and her retinue to infiltrate the Palace directly.<ref name="IoTA4">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]], Adventure 4: Aid the Queen</ref> Rorworr and the other students escaped the Plaza as Royal Security Forces began engaging the Trade Federation Army throughout Theed.<ref name="BitS!">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20001120battle|text=Battle in the Streets!|int=Battle in the Streets!}}</ref>

{{RPG|"[[Battle in the Streets!]]"}}
The students evaded several battle droid patrols and eventually found their way back to the Palace Plaza. A [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droid]], supplemented by four battle droids, had replaced the previous patrol. The droids were firing at the occupants in the Plaza—four unarmed civilians, a pair of RSF Guards, and an RSF pilot with an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]]. Rorworr's unit intervened, eventually securing the Plaza. The pilot, severely wounded, claimed that the astromech droid contained vital information, and charged the students with seeing that it found its way to Queen Amidala. One of the wounded Security Guards likewise charged Rorworr's team with ensuring that a [[diplomatic pouch]] found its way to Ambassador [[Kron]] of Alderaan.<ref name="BitS!" />
{{Endgame}}
[[File:FFTakrab1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Rorworr, Sia-Lan Wezz, and Deel Surool apprehend Gode Takrab and examine his counterfeit holovid.]]
Returning to the Royal Hangar, Rorworr's team ambushed the escort of a Neimoidian counterfeiter, [[Gode Takrab]]. After interrogating Takrab, they learned that he had [[Counterfeit Treaty of Naboo|forged a treaty]] between Naboo and the Trade Federation, as well as doctored a [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] of Queen Amidala endorsing the phony document. Rorworr's team scoured Takrab's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and eventually found the fake agreement<ref name="FF91" /> hidden within Takrab's [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[TC-11]]. Nute Gunray sent a squad of battle droids led by [[RK-7]] to secure the shuttle for his own escape.<ref name="PoNA3" /> Rorworr's unit was nearly overwhelmed, but held the shuttle until the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Droid Control Ship]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the planet was destroyed, neutralizing the droid forces and ending the battle.<ref name="FF91" />

====After the Invasion====
{{Quote|You will always be welcome in my home, wherever it may be. The people of Naboo owe you a tremendous debt of gratitude…|Queen Amidala to the crew from the ''Crescent''|<ref name="PoNE" />}}
{{RPG|"[[Peril on Naboo]]"}}
In gratitude for their services to Naboo during the weeks of occupation, as well as their exemplary performance during the Battle of Naboo, a grateful Queen Amidala bestowed the honorary title of [[Naboo Palace Guard/Legends|Palace Guard]]<ref name="PoNE" /> upon Rorworr, Wezz, Surool, Korden, Galak, and Dané.<ref name="FF91" /> Rorworr spent the rest of the day freeing prisoners from internment camps. The group was visited later in the day by Captain Pargen and his daughter. The ''Crescent'' had narrowly escaped the pirates and battle droids, but was forced to set down under Lake Paonga, where the vessel was eventually rescued by Gungans.<ref name="PoNE" />
{{RPG|''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]''}}
That night, Rorworr and his friends attended a [[Funeral of Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|funeral for Qui-Gon Jinn]], who had been [[Death/Legends|slain]] by Darth Maul in the recent battle. During the ceremony, the students were approached by Lucos Dannt, Sabé, and Lialla Tane. Sabé asked the members of the defunct Underground to investigate a group of Naboo and Gungan refugees camped in some of Naboo's ancient ruins. The leader of the refugees was rumored to have plans against the throne, and intended to disrupt the Naboo-Gungan alliance. Tane, who had originally reported the camp, accompanied the team as a guide, and the unit set out in a speeder. When a large, falling [[Tree/Legends|tree]] forced the speeder to a halt, the craft and its occupants were assaulted by a large, powerfully built Naboo predator known as a [[grakkt]]. The venomous creature disabled the group's speeder before being driven off, forcing the team to continue their mission on foot. At one point, Tane tumbled into a large pool of [[Quicksand/Legends|quicksand]], and had to be rescued.<ref name="Renegade">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]], Adventure 5: Renegade</ref>

The group soldiered on through the swamp and came across a [[dark grove]] [[Vergence/Legends|corrupted]] by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. A [[mutated tangler tree]] at the center of the grove attacked the team with vines and projectile thorns until they were out of reach. Just outside the refugee camp, the group was attacked by a [[Mind trick/Legends#Force illusion|Force illusion]] of Darth Maul, secretly conjured by the renegade leader to deter the party from continuing. After overcoming the illusory Sith Lord, Rorworr and his friends discovered the refugee camp hidden among ancient Gungan ruins and were confronted by several of the renegades. Three of the refugees in particular, a [[Unidentified tough-looking woman|tough-looking woman]], a [[Unidentified Gungan warrior|Gungan warrior]], and an [[Unidentified angry young man|angry young man]], condemned Amidala while praising their leader, a charismatic man by the name of [[Savor Kibbs]], who was secretly a rejected Jedi Padawan. Kibbs, finally determining that the intruders must be eliminated, attacked the group with [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] and a reactivated droideka. The party defeated Kibbs and ended the hypnotic influence he exerted over his followers.<ref name="Renegade" />

{{RPG|"[[Peril in the Ionosphere]]"}}
Sometime after the invasion was settled, Rorworr joined a project<ref name="PitI">{{GamerCite|1|Peril in the Ionosphere}}</ref> spearheaded by Queen Amidala, Master Kenobi, and Boss Nass<ref name="Star Wars Episode I: The Gungan Frontier">[[Star Wars Episode I: The Gungan Frontier|''Star Wars'' Episode I: ''The Gungan Frontier'']]</ref> to help develop a Gungan colony on Naboo's [[Water/Legends|water]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]],<ref name="PitI" /> [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]].<ref>"[[Peril in the Ionosphere]]" describes the project's destination only as "Naboo's large water moon." However, the adventure was released in [[2000]], little more than a year after the release of ''[[Star Wars Episode I: The Gungan Frontier]]'', which identifies Ohma-D'un as a colony site. Although ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' identifies another of Naboo's moons, [[Rori/Legends|Rori]], and establishes a Gungan colony on it, this did not occur until [[2003]]. Additionally, {{CSWECite|III|335|Worlohp, Captain}} confirms that Captain Worlohp's regular route is to Ohma-D'un.</ref> At the [[Naboo Civic Spaceport]], Rorworr boarded a small [[Republic Sienar Systems/Legends|Republic Sienar Systems]] [[Short Range Transit Shuttle SST-67|shuttle]], the ''[[Fair Gale]]'', captained by [[Worlohp]], an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]]. The other passengers—a pair of graduates of the [[Theed School of Technology and Engineering]], [[Kharl Vanneb]] and [[Sakme Kelene]]; Professor [[Hallem Celaar]], a renowned zoological and [[Botanist/Legends|botanical]] expert;<ref name="PitI" /> as well as Toba<ref>A caption within ''[[Peril in the Ionosphere]]''{{'s}} introduction indicates that the adventure uses characters from ''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]''. An image in PitI depicts a Gungan trying to wrestle with the ''Fair Gale''{{'s}} controls. As Toba is the only playable Gungan from the Invasion of Theed Adventure game, and as Toba's short Wizards biography mentions he's one of the first Gungans chosen to colonize Ohma-D'un, he is assumed to be the depicted Gungan.</ref> and a few of Rorworr's classmates from the Royal House of Learning—were also all part of the project.<ref name="PitI" />

Sixteen [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] after takeoff, the ''Fair Gale'' collided with an object in Naboo's ionosphere. The impact ruptured the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] conduit, which had begun leaking [[Kyvalon-4]], a gas used in many [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] systems. The gas, while harmless to most [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], caused temporary homicidal insanity in Ithorians. Captain Worlohp entered the passenger bay to report colliding with debris from the Trade Federation Droid Control Ship that had been destroyed during the invasion. After breathing the Kyvalon-4, the Ithorian flew into a rage and attacked his passengers. The ensuing melee damaged several of the ship's vital systems, including its [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] and [[Life support/Legends|life support]] systems. The passengers subdued Worlohp; repaired the navigation, life support, and repulsor systems; and landed the shuttle safely.<ref name="PitI" />

[[File:WookieeFlyingLessons.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Rorworr demonstrates a Wookiee's strength while fighting alongside Sia-Lan Wezz.]]
Rorworr stayed on with the Ohma-D'un project for a [[Time/Legends|time]], earning a monthly salary of 2,000 credits and joint ownership of the ''[[Mystic Burn]]'',<ref name="PitI" /> a [[YT-1200 medium freighter|YT-1200 light freighter]]<ref name="Shipbuilding Secrets">{{GamerCite|1|Shipbuilding Secrets}}</ref> stored at the colony's moonbase.<ref name="PitI" />
{{Endgame}}

===Republic service===
{{Quote|For the Separatists!|A Human thug before attempting to gun down Senator Alastar Treen in a Corellian spaceport cantina|<ref name="RCRB&SCRB">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]'' and ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''</ref>}}
As Rorworr grew into an adult, his heroism on Naboo eventually led him to serve the Republic as a scout. He often went wherever the Republic needed his help, and generally ended up on small wilderness planets due to his skills in survival and exploration.<ref name="RIS" /> At one point in the Wookiee's career, the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor's]] [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|office]] assigned Rorworr and former members of the Naboo Underground to investigate an island cove hidden on a [[Unidentified jungle planet (Blue Star Pirates)|jungle planet]]. Their goal was to discover who was supplying the [[Blue Star Pirates]] with military-grade weapons. After defeating a pair of pirates within the cove, the investigation team discovered a datacard from the [[Hyperdrive Cantina]], a floating [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] on the water planet [[Vorusku]]. The team followed the lead and subdued a group of thugs within the cantina. Then, having learned that the supplier was a local Vorusku [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], they tried to ascertain his identity, which led them into an ambush in a [[Volcano/Legends|volcano]] city by a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. After Rorworr and his partners acquired the identity of the supplier, [[Jalla Spree]], Spree sent a droideka to attack the investigation team at a cliffside [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] camp.<ref name="AGRB" />

In the wake of the Invasion of Naboo, several planets and corporations formed an alliance known as the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. These systems seceded from the Republic in protest over perceived corruption within the Republic Senate. This resulted in several brutal skirmishes across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]],<ref name="SWE2">{{Film|II}}</ref> including the [[Battle of Ohma-D'un|devastation of the Ohma-D'un colony]],<ref name="Star Wars: Republic: The New Face of War">''[[Star Wars: Republic: The New Face of War]]''</ref> and by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] the galaxy was drawn into a full-scale conflict known as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="SWE2" />

Weeks prior to the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], the first official battle of the Clone Wars, Rorworr, Sia-Lan Wezz, and Deel Surool met [[Vor'en Kurn]],<ref name="RCRB&SCRB" /><ref>{In ''The First Game Session'', an example scenario set in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the Gamemaster (Michelle) says, "You've all noticed each other, but you don't know each other yet." (''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]'', ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''.) This conflicts with the established idea that Rorworr, Sia-Lan Wezz, and Deel Surool worked together in the Naboo Underground. For the purposes of this article, it is assumed that the GM means the other characters don't know Vor'en Kurn, yet.</ref> a blue-skinned [[Human/Legends|Human]] soldier,<ref name="Kurn">Vor'en Kurn is listed as "Human" in all three Wizards of the Coast Core Rulebooks. However, his image in the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'' is color shifted, displaying Kurn with blue skin. He is depicted with blue skin again in ''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'', and the skin color is confirmed in the text within ''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''.</ref> while in a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] cantina on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. There, the four foiled a Separatist assassination attempt on a [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]], [[Alastar Treen]].<ref name="RCRB&SCRB" /> Following this, the four frequently worked together. The team faced a variety of opponents and situations, such as attacks on Battle Droid commanders, the investigation of a bounty hunter operating out of the [[Royal Hutt Hotel]], assaults on crime bosses, encounters with [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] on Tatooine,<ref name="CRB&RCRB" /> cantina brawls with [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]]<ref name="RPGCRB" /> and Rodian gangs,<ref name="RPGRCRB">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]''</ref> an escape from a battle droid [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]],<ref name="CRB&RCRB" /> an escape from a [[Colicoid/Legends|Colicoid]]-run prison in the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|Spice Mines of Kessel]],<ref name="GOI" /> a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] ambush in an abandoned armory, and even [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|mounted blasters]] in the darkened corridors of the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Grumbog]]'s [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Grumbog's space station fortress|fortress]].<ref name="CRB&RCRB" />

[[File:WookieeToss.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Rorworr, Vor'en Kurn, and Sia-Lan Wezz deal with a battle droid ambush in close quarters.]]
The team was so successful that they occasionally received orders directly from the office of the Chancellor and the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. One such mission had the group investigating the disappearance of a Republic arsenal ship that had been tasked with decommissioning the Trade Federation Army defeated at Naboo. The investigation led the team to [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]], where they defeated several re-activated battle droids.<ref name="RPGCRB" />

Another mission to which the team was assigned involved preventing [[Corann]] from being the first of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to withdraw from the Republic. Rorworr's team investigated rumors of Separatist activity on the small industrial planet and discovered a droid manufacturing plant.<ref name="RPGRCRB" /> Another mission saw Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] personally assign the team to investigate a [[Sith temple (Puloorn)|Sith temple]] on the snow-covered planet of [[Puloorn]]. Along the way, they encountered marauders and navigated through challenging [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid fields]]. Within the temple itself, they discovered dark side beasts and [[Sith cult/Legends|cultists]].<ref name="CRB&RCRB" />

Over the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the Wookiee made many recommendations regarding the development of new worlds, which the Republic often ignored. On occasion, credit for some of Rorworr's discoveries went to more [[Politics/Legends|politically]] connected Humans. Rorworr also earned two official reprimands during his time with the Republic. He was issued one for disappearing from an exploration party, during which time he found a [[Unidentified civilization (Rorworr's find)|lost civilization]]. On another mission, he earned a reprimand for not revealing a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Unidentified sentient life form|life form]] he discovered, at that life form's request. Rorworr grew increasingly disenfranchised with the Republic as a result, a sentiment that he noticed was mirrored by many other Wookiees and [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] species. However, Rorworr kept quiet regarding his concerns; instead, the Wookiee spent his spare time listening to stories and tales in [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] bars, trying to learn of unexplored worlds and territories.<ref name="RIS" />

===Rebel Wookiee===
{{Quote|I knew it! You ''are'' smugglers! Troopers, arrest them!|Old Ezrim to Rorworr and his team at Tosche Station|<ref name="ANaTS">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20010621a2|text=A Night at Tosche Station|int=A Night at Tosche Station}}</ref>}}
The establishment of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] changed Rorworr's life dramatically. Most of his clan was lost in an Imperial raid, the Wookiee's own father vanished after speaking out against the newly rechristened [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine,<ref name="RRA" /> and Queen Amidala was slain. Anakin Skywalker, the boy Rorworr had met while recapturing Theed,<ref name="PoNA2" /> became Darth Vader, Palpatine's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]]. Many of the Jedi Rorworr had worked with were massacred in a synchronous, galaxy-wide execution known as [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith">{{Film|III}}</ref> The surviving Jedi were pursued by Darth Vader, and many, including Rorworr's close friends, Sia-Lan Wezz<ref name="SWP">[[Purge (comic book)|''Purge'']]</ref> and Rann I-Kanu, respectively were killed or went missing.<ref name="FoCC">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20010913a|text=The Fall of Cloud City|int=The Fall of Cloud City}}</ref> Rorworr felt betrayed by the Republic he had served, and quickly joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] to resist the Empire's reign.<ref name="RRA" />

In the Rebellion, Rorworr was often tasked with monitoring Imperial ship and troop movements. Often, he flew a Rebel [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]], having become adept at hiding the craft in asteroid fields. Other times, he dealt directly with Imperials, taking advantage of the stereotype of Wookiees as slow, simple creatures in order to be underestimated. On the ground, Rorworr spent time in cantinas pretending to be a fuzzy drunk, eavesdropping on soldiers that did not realize he understood Basic. In one case, he took a job as a freighter pilot running rare foods to Imperial forces on [[Varlinaar]], and in the process brought with him a team of Rebels to steal Imperial battle plans.<ref name="RRA" /> In another, he freed an imprisoned Rebel operative from an Imperial detention cell.<ref name="RPGSCRB">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''</ref>

{{RPG|"[[The Fall of Cloud City]]"}}

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Rorworr reunited with other members of the defunct Naboo Underground for a vacation to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. At the conclusion of their holiday, while the friends waited for a shuttle to arrive and deliver them to an [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger liner]], a group of thugs dragged an old man from the waiting lobby onto the landing platform. A small astromech droid disrupted the thugs, and the old man quickly asked for the group's help. After intervening, Rorworr and his friends learned that the thugs had been hired by a [[Chevin/Legends|Chevin]] working for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]; that the old man was [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Shemza]], a distinguished [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] wanted by the Empire for his research; and that the astromech, [[Shorty (droid)|Shorty]], belonged to Shemza's Rebellion [[Shemza's Rebel contact|contact]]. Shemza insisted that he had information vital to the Rebellion, and asked the group to accompany him to his contact's transport on [[Landing Platform 21]]. The voice of [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], Cloud City's [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]], broke over the city's loudspeakers, announcing that the Empire had [[Occupation of Bespin|occupied the city]], and recommended that all citizens evacuate. Three [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] appeared in the hallway, demanding that Rorworr's group halt. The team defeated the stormtroopers and escaped Cloud City with Doctor Shemza, his Alliance contact, and Shorty.<ref name="FoCC" /><ref name="The Fall of Cloud City">As the adventure, ''[[The Fall of Cloud City]]'', ends, it suggests that maybe the Heroes join the Rebellion, or maybe they venture with Shemza to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]].</ref>

{{RPG|"[[A Night at Tosche Station]]"}}

Rorworr's team participated in a mission to purchase [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] for the Rebellion from a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] smuggler, [[Hagk Baht]], on Tatooine. The group arrived outside of the town of [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]], but a particularly violent sandstorm forced the group to take shelter inside of [[Tosche Station/Legends|Tosche Station]] while waiting for their contact. A squad of Imperial stormtroopers also took refuge within the mechanic's bar. A local [[Pacithhip/Legends|Pacithhip]] [[Erkwil|spice dealer]] tried to hide his stash on the Rebels, while [[Ezrim|Old Ezrim]], a local [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]], began to insist that the group were spice dealers. The argument was interrupted when Baht broke over the team's [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and explained that, although he had escaped an [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] ship by entering the sandstorm, he had little control over his starship inside the maelstrom. Baht's transmission was cut short as his ship, the ''[[Singing Savrip]]'', crashed into the desert. The stormtroopers moved to arrest the Rebels, but were defeated with the help of the lounge's mechanic, [[Laze Loneozner/Legends|Fixer]], a pilot by the name of [[Verzon Tennd]], and his droid, [[R2-D9|Deenine]]. When the team investigated the crash site of the ''Singing Savrip'', they discovered that Baht had been killed in the impact, but fifty rifles remained intact. Rorworr secured the rifles for the Rebellion.<ref name="ANaTS" />
{{Endgame}}

At some point, Rorworr, Arani Korden, and Deel Surool participated in the [[Battle of Skorrupon]], intercepting [[High Inquisitor]] [[Volytar]] at his private retreat on [[Skorrupon/Legends|Skorrupon]], as he tried to board a shuttle and escape to his orbiting [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. After battling Volytar and eight destroyer droids in a room filled with pop-up grenade traps, the three became known as the heroes of Skorrupon among the Rebellion, but Rorworr was devastated to receive confirmation of Sia-Lan Wezz's death.<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref><ref>A line in ''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]'': "Rorworr takes his ship on a grand tour of the galaxy, eventually falling into obscurity as he tries to forget the loss of Sia-Lan at the battle of Skorrupon," conflicts with the later published [[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']] regarding the death of Sia-Lan Wezz. For the purposes of this article, the line is interpreted as: "At the battle of Skorrupon, Rorworr decides to take his ship on a grand tour of the galaxy, eventually falling into obscurity as he tries to forget the loss of Sia-Lan."</ref>

===Independent explorer===
After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Rorworr passed up service in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the nascent government established by the Rebel Alliance, to become an independent explorer.<ref name="RSL">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010614b3_rorworr&c=rpg|text=Rorworr, Rebel Agent}}</ref> Hoping to put some of his grief behind him,<ref name="GCG" /> Rorworr quietly began exploring the galaxy alone, piloting a modified [[Svelte-class Imperial shuttle|''Svelte''-class shuttle]] painted with a camouflage scheme.<ref name="RSL" />

The Wookiee retained several contacts from his time with the Rebellion, and often tapped them when he encountered problems that he felt needed attention. Occasionally, young Jedi or New Republic operatives were assigned to help Rorworr by their superiors. Rorworr gave the most attention to situations where organizations or individuals sought to desecrate the wilder planets or enslave their inhabitants. A great number of worlds produced stories about how Rorworr brought down a [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] factory, helped evacuate a threatened animal population, foiled a Hutt slaving ring, or performed some other altruistic task. [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] had a particular fondness for the Wookiee, and Rorworr traveled to [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], the Ewok [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], several times in his life.<ref name="RSL" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:ThreeWookiees.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Left to right: Rorworr, Chewbacca, and [[Tyvokka/Legends|Tyvokka]]]]
{{Dialogue|Sia-Lan|When Deel goes for the door, we'll have to cover him!|Rorworr|'''OOORRAWK!'''|TD0-2|He says—|Sia-Lan|No need to translate, I caught the tone.|attr=Rorworr gets his point across|src=<ref name="BfT" />}}
Rorworr was a confident, courageous,<ref name="Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook">''Heart'', a Scout class feature. ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]''</ref> and intimidating Wookiee with an insatiable curiosity and wanderlust.<ref name="RIS" /> He was eager to perform acts of heroism,<ref name="IoTAG" /> but had grown more cynical as the Galactic Republic was reshaped into the Empire.<ref name="RRA" /> Due to his actions, "Rorworr" became a household name across Naboo<ref name="Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook">[https://web.archive.org/web/20050426024932/www.wizards.com/starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010614b4_rorworr&c=rpg Rorworr's RPG stats] give him a reputation score of 4. Table 6-6 of the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' rates reputation scores from 3 to 5 as "Known on home world"</ref> and produced stories of heroism on over fifty other worlds.<ref name="RSL" /> The Wookiee found humor in the fact that, despite looking and smelling completely different, he was occasionally mistaken for [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], another famous Wookiee in the Rebellion. If it served to benefit him or the Rebellion, Rorworr would not correct the misidentification.<ref name="RRA" /> Rorworr was a very expressive Wookiee and was capable of letting out a mournful hoot<ref name="GOI" /> or a displeased roar.<ref name="BfT" /> Rorworr disliked it when he was forced to leave behind temporary allies,<ref name="GOI" /> but he cherished his friendships and was devastated whenever he lost any of his longtime companions.<ref name="GCG" /> Although Rorworr often took a liking to young, idealistic heroes, later in life he preferred his solitude and rarely remained with any one group for long.<ref name="RSL" />

In [[Shyriiwook/Legends|Shyriiwook]], the name prefix "Ror" translated to "dark, hidden, marsh, swamp," while the suffix "warr" translated to "forest, guide, hunter, scout."<ref name="CoK">{{GamerCite|10|Callsigns: Names of Kashyyyk}}</ref>

===Skills and abilities===
At 2.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall<ref name="CRB&RCRB" /> and 140 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]],<ref name="Kilos" /> Rorworr was a strong and able-bodied scout<ref name="RSL" /> and gunslinger.<ref name="RPGSCRB" /> His years of evading predators on Kashyyyk aided his ability to move quietly and remain out of sight,<ref name="GOI" /> while his natural Wookiee strength allowed him to [[Dismemberment/Legends|dismember]] battle droids with his bare hands<ref name="BfT" /> or hurl humanoids with a single arm.<ref name="SoC">{{WizCite|story=Shadows of Coruscant|book=Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook}}</ref> He was able to react quickly to danger<ref>In the ''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]'', Rorworr has the second highest Initiative score of all the characters presented. In ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'', the Gunslinger class gains Improved Quick Draw, which allows a character to draw and fire a weapon in the first round of combat, even when surprised.</ref> and was quick to recover from it. Rorworr was an accomplished mechanic, slicer, pilot, [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogator]], and pathfinder. The Wookiee could fly freighters with ease,<ref name="MRWS">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010614b_rorworr&c=rpg|text=Meet Rorworr: Wookiee Scout}}</ref> while his N-1 training on Naboo<ref name="RIS" /> allowed him to deftly maneuver most starfighters.<ref name="RSL" />

Rorworr was proficient in [[Wrruushi/Legends|martial arts]], as well as the use of [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], blaster rifles, most simple weapons, and the [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]], a traditional Wookiee weapon. Although he favored the bowcaster,<ref name="RSL" /> Rorworr had become an adept quick-draw with blaster pistols.<ref name="RPGSCRB" /> As a Wookiee, Rorworr was capable of speaking only Shyriiwook, although he easily understood Basic, [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]], [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], [[Mon Calamari (language)/Legends|Mon Calamarian]], and [[Twi'leki/Legends|Twi'leki]] without the help of his translator droid.<ref name="RSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Adam came up with this early sketch which really captured what I wanted to see. He didn't look at all like Chewbacca, but he was definitely a Wookiee! My favorite feature is the 'soul patch' on his chin. It really holds the youthful look together.|Sean Glenn|<ref name="PPArtGal">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=pre_art_gallery|text=Preproduction Art Gallery}}</ref>}}
Rorworr was created by [[Wizards of the Coast]] to be an iconic representation of the Scout class, a character role built around space and on-foot exploration, for their [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] line. As an iconic character, Rorworr would act as an illustration reference for future supplements as well as provide viewers with the feeling that they are following the Wookiee's adventures. His early visual design was created by [[Adam Hughes]] and implemented in ''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]''.<ref name="PPArtGal" /> [[Doug Alexander Gregory]] was tasked with aging the design by a few years for inclusion in the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'', and accomplished the goal after asking questions like, "What does a Wookiee's skull look like?" TD0-2 was created by [[Bill Slavicsek]], who saw the need for Rorworr to speak to his party without breaking suspension of disbelief. Rorworr became one of Art Director [[Sean Glenn]]'s favorite iconic characters.<ref name="PArtGal">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=rpg_production_gallery|text=Production Art Gallery}}</ref>
[[File:RorworrActionFigure.jpg|thumb|left|101px|Rorworr's official action figure]]
A 4" Rorworr [[Hasbro]] action figure was included in the box for the ''Invasion of Theed Adventure Game'' as an exclusive part of Hasbro's ''[[Star Wars: Power of the Jedi]]'' toy line. The figure featured six points of articulation, and a bowcaster accessory.<ref name="RebScum">{{Rebelscum|url=potjinvoftheed.asp|text='''RORWORR''' (WOOKIEE SCOUT) INVASION OF THEED Adventure Game}}</ref> While the figure's head was a new sculpt, an older [[Kenner Products]] toy mold, [https://www.rebelscum.com/POTF2chewieboussh.asp Chewbacca as [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh's]] bounty] from the [[Star Wars: The Power of the Force (1995 toy line)]] series, was re-used for Rorworr's body. Despite being a Hasbro release, the figure retained the Kenner stamp on the bottom of one of its feet.<ref name="RebScumFoot">[https://www.rebelscum.com/story/front/rorworrs_foot_4366.asp Rebelscum.com; '''Rorworr's Foot''']; Posted by Dustin on December 2, 2000 at 08:14 PM CST</ref> Rorworr was the first ''[[Star Wars]]'' character from a non-computer-based [[roleplaying game]] to be rendered as an action figure.

Rorworr is often used in examples within Wizards of the Coast sourcebooks, and has been documented as having been played by "Penny,"<ref name="CRB&RCRB" /> and "Mike."<ref name="RCRB&SCRB" /><ref name="GOI" /> In [[November]] [[2000]], during the Roleplaying Game's launch event in [[Wikipedia:Seattle|Seattle]], [[Peter Mayhew]], Chewbacca's costumed actor, played a demonstration game as Rorworr.<ref name="SFSRPGL">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20001114.html|text=Snapshots from Seattle RPG Launch}}</ref>

In [[2001]], five Wizards of the Coast original ''Star Wars'' characters received three-page biographical articles on [[Wizards.com]]. Each article provided a brief history and gameplay stats at three different points in each character's life. The first was Dané, in [[February]].<ref name="Dane">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010215c2|text=Dané, Customs Security Agent}}</ref> Toba, was next, in [[March]],<ref name="Toba">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010308a&c=rpg|text=Meet Toba: Soldier and Scout}}</ref> then Kelko in [[April]],<ref name="Kelko">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010405b_kelko&c=rpg|text=Meet Kelko: Rodian Fringer}}</ref> and the human [[Force Adept]], [[Valara Saar]], in [[May]].<ref name="Saar">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010517b_valara&c=rpg|text=Meet Valara Saar: Force Adept}}</ref> In [[June]], Rorworr was the last original Wizards of the Coast iconic character to receive a web article, written by [[Jeff Grubb]].<ref name="MRWS" />

In [[2003]], the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]] and set in [[3956 BBY]], was released. One side quest in the game charged players with locating a minor Wookiee character sharing the name [[Rorworr (Kashyyyk)|Rorworr]]. Though this Rorworr was only found as a corpse, it was revealed that Wookiee had captured several fellow Wookiees and sold them as slaves.<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>

In the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'', the former [[Fringer/Legends|fringer]] [[Kelko]] replaces Rorworr as the iconic Scout, while Rorworr was pictured as the iconic Gunslinger, a prestige class.<ref name="RPGSCRB" /> Rorworr was still used as both a Scout and a Gunslinger in examples of play.<ref name="GOI" />

===RPG elements===
Roleplaying game adventures are, by design, typically written to compensate for the unpredictable nature of player actions. For all roleplaying game adventures, this article assumes that the heroes follow the general outline and specific goals described in the adventures, without deviating dramatically.

This article assumes that all example scenarios of Rorworr in the Star Wars Roleplaying Game's ''[[Core Rulebook]]'', its successors, and their supplements are official within the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] continuity.

===="Confirmed" adventures====
[[File:Gametiles2.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Two examples of Rorworr game tiles]]
This article assumes Rorworr's participation as a player character in the ''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game'' and all supplemental Adventure Games that indicate that they were designed to use characters, rules, game tokens, and other assets from ''Invasion of Theed''. These adventures include ''[[The Predators]]'', ''[[The Smugglers of Naboo|Smugglers of Naboo Fast-Play Game]]'',<ref name="TP&SON" /> ''[[Signal Interruption]]'',<ref name="SI" /> ''[[The Fall of Cloud City]]'',<ref name="FoCC" /> ''[[A Night at Tosche Station]]'',<ref name="ANaTS" /> ''[[Peril in the Ionosphere]]'',<ref name="PitI" /> and ''[[Battle in the Streets!]]''<ref name="BitS!" />

Rorworr is never mentioned by name in ''[[Secrets of Naboo]]'' or the included adventure, ''[[Peril on Naboo]]''. However, an image appearing near the end of the adventure depicts the visually distinct Wookiee, alongside Sia-Lan Wezz and Deel Surool, apprehending Gode Takrab and examining his forged holovid, suggesting that Rorworr was one of the heroes in the adventure.<ref name="PoNA3" /> Rorworr's participation was later confirmed by ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 91]]'', published by [[De Agostini]].<ref name="FF91" />

====Further adventures====
Rorworr was used in a live example game with celebrity players during the Seattle RPG Launch Event. Rorworr (Peter Mayhew) accompanies [[Dorn Tavers]] ([[Jake Lloyd]]),<ref name="SFSRPGL" /> Deel Surool ([[Drew Struzan]]), and Vor'en Kurn (Adam Hughes).<ref name="TFNLPC">[http://www.theforce.net/rpg/story/the_seattle_star_wars_society_reports_74554.asp TheForce.Net; '''The Seattle Star Wars Society Reports'''; Posted By JF on November 15, 2000]. A [http://www.theforce.net/rpg/newspics/photo9.jpg photograph] allows clear identification of the player's character sheets.</ref> Dorn Tavers attacked a group of non-humans without warning, leaving Rorworr and the others to back him up.<ref name="SFSRPGL" /> However, Tavers soon turned on his allies, too.<ref name="JakeDarkSide">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=rpg_wp_jake|text=Jake Lloyd: Fast Track to the Dark Side?}}</ref>

Rorworr is depicted in an illustration among the pages of ''[[Shadows of Coruscant]]'', an adventure printed in the ''Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook''; however, the illustration does not appear to depict a scene from the adventure.<ref name="SoC" />

''A Night at Tosche Station'', an Adventure Game/Fast Play supplement set during the [[Rebellion era]], indicates that it can be linked to the end of a previous Roleplaying Game supplement, ''[[The Storm's Edge]]'', by changing that adventure's setting to be near Anchorhead rather than near [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]].<ref name="ANaTS" />

A Wookiee that appears to be Rorworr, along with figures that appear to be Arani Korden and Rann I-Kanu, is pictured on the wrap-around cover of ''[[Tempest Feud]]'', a large adventure written by Jeff Grubb and [[Owen K.C. Stephens]] and published in March [[2002]] by Wizards of the Coast. However, Rorworr is not depicted within the adventure,<ref name="TmpFd">''[[Tempest Feud]]''.</ref> [[Scourge (novel)|''Scourge'']], a novelization of ''Tempest Feud'' written by Jeff Grubb, was released in [[2012]] without any references to Rorworr.<ref name="Scourge">[[Scourge (novel)|''Scourge'']]</ref>

The web article "Meet Rorworr: Wookiee Scout" differs from the previous four write-ups of Wizards-original characters. "Meet Rorworr" was credited to Jeff Grubb,<ref name="MRWS" /> while the others were written by [[Cory J. Herndon]]. All of the articles include the tag, "Look for [this character]'s equipment in our equipment image gallery!" However, while the other characters' articles twice list, "Use [This character] in a new adventure based on our Adventure Hook Generator,"<ref name="Dane" /><ref name="Toba" /><ref name="Kelko" /><ref name="Saar" /> Rorworr's articles are tagged with: "Make Rorworr your hero in our [[The Smugglers of Naboo|Star Wars fast-play game]]!" and "Play Rorworr in your ''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]'' campaign!"<ref name="MRWS" /> While ''[[Between Sand and Sky]]'' is the only campaign that was packaged with the supplement, ''Secrets of Tatooine'' is a sourcebook designed to help develop or create any adventure that involves Tatooine.<ref name="SOT">''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]''</ref>

===Discrepancies===
Text in ''The Official Star Wars Fact File 91'' confirms Rorworr, Surool, Galak, Korden and "Dane" as a part of Mett Habble's Naboo Resistance. While it is likely that Fact File refers to the handmaiden "Dané," all of the images that have captions referencing "Dane" instead depict the Jedi Sia-Lan Wezz.<ref name="FF91" /> The error is reprinted in [[The Official Star Wars Fact File Part 33|Part 33]] of De Agostini's [[2014]] [[The Official Star Wars Fact File (2014)|revised run of The Official Star Wars Fact File]].<ref name="2014FF33">{{FactFile2014|33|32 BBY 69-70|The Battle of Naboo}}</ref>

Although Rorworr is noted as a participating member in the operations of both Mett Habble's Naboo Resistance<ref name="FF91" /> and Lucos Dannt's Naboo Underground,<ref name="RIS" /> the two organizations otherwise appear to be unaffiliated resistance cells. Mett Habble informs the heroes of Queen Amidala's escape to Coruscant within hours of Theed's invasion, and the heroes accompany Habble to a meeting with the Queen soon after her ship lands in the swamp.<ref name="PoNA2" /> However, members of the Naboo Underground are told that the fate of the Queen is still unknown late into the occupation.<ref name="IoTA3" /> In addition, the Heroes of the Underground are aimlessly patrolling occupied Theed, seemly unaware that the Queen has returned to Naboo, when they encounter the disguised Sabé within Theed as the second battle for the capital begins.<ref name="IoTA4" />

''Peril on Naboo'' indicates that the strategy meeting with Queen Amidala's forces, the Gungans, and the Naboo Resistance takes place at the landing site for the Queen's royal starship.<ref name="PoNA2" /> However, most other iterations of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] indicate that the meeting takes place elsewhere, after the Trade Federation forces have located and secured the Queen's ship.<ref name="E1TPM" />

In ''The First Game Session'', an example scenario from the ''Roleplaying Game: Saga Edition Core Rulebook'' set weeks prior to the Battle of Geonosis, the Gamemaster "Michelle" says, "You've all noticed each other, but you don't know each other yet."<ref name="RCRB&SCRB" /> This conflicts with the established idea that Rorworr, Sia-Lan Wezz, and Deel Surool worked together in the Naboo Underground<ref name="IoTAG" /> and the Naboo Resistance.<ref name="FF91" /> This article assumes that the GM means the characters don't know Vor'en Kurn yet.

A line on page 160 of ''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]'' says, "Rorworr takes his ship on a grand tour of the galaxy, eventually falling into obscurity as he tries to forget the loss of Sia-Lan at the battle of Skorrupon."<ref name="GCG" /> Although Wezz is pictured in [[:File:RebelPoster.jpg|in-universe propaganda]] after the destruction of Alderaan,<ref name="HG">''[[Hero's Guide]]''</ref> the concept that Wezz was killed during the Battle of Skorrupon conflicts with the later published [[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']], which illustrates the young Jedi being slain by Darth Vader on Kessel within months of Order 66.<ref name="SWP" /> For the purposes of this article, the line is interpreted as: "At the battle of Skorrupon, Rorworr decides to take his ship on a grand tour of the galaxy, eventually falling into obscurity as he tries to forget the loss of Sia-Lan."
